confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_81ccc610-1a0c-4e59-81a3-e960332c3a92 read back as promoted

payload.authority_note: Operator refined the operator-surface queue proposal with visibility-gated delivery and explicit undelivered policy.
envelope_requirements: recipient identity and optional target surface binding
delivery mode such as when_visible or immediate_if_visible

problem.desired_behavior: Submission returns queued/awaiting_visible_surface. A delivery worker attempts delivery only when target HWND is visible/focusable, with confirmation, retry, and governed fallback.
problem.incorrect_behavior: Synchronous delivery either fails, types into the wrong place, or depends on current desktop visibility.
problem.scenario: Operator asks architect to nudge another agent, then switches Windows virtual desktops before delivery. The target window may exist but be hidden/cloaked on another desktop.

payload.proposed_invariant: Message authority is Site/user-level. Delivery opportunity is PC/window/desktop-level. A durable promise bridges them without collapsing them.

requests.text: Add delivery_promise records for operator-surface messages: queued, awaiting_visible_surface, delivery_attempted, delivered_to_surface, admitted_by_recipient, timed_out, fallback_routed, reconciled.
Add visibility-gated delivery semantics: target HWND must exist, be visible/focusable, not minimized/cloaked, and input escrow must be available before keystroke delivery.

Add explicit undelivered policy to message envelopes with typed timeout actions such as route_to_recipient_inbox, route_to_sender_inbox, notify_sender, cancel, escalate_to_operator, retry_until_session_end, and require_manual_delivery.
Ensure fallback routing creates another governed inbox/message artifact rather than silently dropping the original message.
Document the scale distinction: queue authority is Site/user-level and shared across desktops; delivery attempt is PC/window/desktop-local and opportunistic.

payload.summary: Operator-surface messages must be higher-level than Windows virtual desktops. Sending should enqueue a durable message and return a delivery promise; PC-local delivery should attempt only when the target surface is visible/focusable. The envelope must define what happens if delivery is not made within a bounded time.
payload.title: Extend operator-surface message queue with delivery promises and timeout fallback policy
